 {"id":"git_safety_guard-a9g","title":"[PACK] backup.rclone - Rclone Pack","description":"# Rclone Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Rclone cloud sync operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### CRITICAL: sync vs copy
- `rclone sync` - DELETES files in dest not in source!
- This is the #1 rclone footgun

### Other destructive
- `rclone delete` - Deletes files matching pattern
- `rclone purge` - Deletes path and all contents
- `rclone cleanup` - Removes incomplete uploads
- `rclone dedupe` - Can delete duplicates
- `rclone move` - Deletes source after copy

## Safe Patterns
- `rclone copy` - Only copies, no deletion
- `rclone ls`, `rclone lsd`, `rclone lsl`
- `rclone size`
- `rclone check`
- `--dry-run` flag on any command
- `rclone config`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: backup.* (git_safety_guard-alc)
- Keywords: rclone
- CRITICAL: Prominently warn about sync vs copy

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] sync command blocked (major footgun)
- [ ] delete/purge blocked
- [ ] copy allowed
- [ ] --dry-run recognized as safe modifier
- [ ] Tests specifically cover sync vs copy","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:32:52.784475336-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T18:44:48.508802304-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T18:44:48.508802304-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-a9g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:30.886445889-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-a9g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:30.9244587-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-a9g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:30.9642347-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-a9g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.027692817-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-a9g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.070922234-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-a9g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.110259888-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-a9g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:08.565056815-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-a9g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:08.609180299-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-aac","title":"[PACK] messaging.nats - NATS/JetStream Pack","description":"# NATS/JetStream Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for NATS CLI operations.

## Destructive Patterns
- `nats stream delete` / `nats stream rm`
- `nats stream purge`
- `nats consumer delete` / `nats consumer rm`
- `nats kv del` / `nats kv rm`
- `nats object delete`
- `nats account delete`

## Safe Patterns
- `nats stream info`, `nats stream ls`
- `nats consumer info`, `nats consumer ls`
- `nats kv get`, `nats kv ls`
- `nats pub`, `nats sub`
- `nats server info`
- `nats bench`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: messaging.* (git_safety_guard-me0)
- Keywords: nats
- Note: JetStream operations are persistent

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Stream deletion blocked
- [ ] Purge operations blocked
- [ ] KV store deletion handled
- [ ] Tests cover common NATS operations","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:31:55.296715544-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T18:35:08.855785045-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T18:35:08.855785045-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-aac","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:32.813656475-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-aac","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:32.852566495-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-aac","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:32.89066112-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-aac","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:32.930531667-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-aac","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:32.998651807-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-aac","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:33.043153327-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-aac","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:09.307648769-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-aac","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:09.354280927-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-advk","title":"TUI/CLI Visual Polish & Rich Terminal Output","description":"# Epic: TUI/CLI Visual Polish & Rich Terminal Output

## Priority Score: 720/1000 (High Priority)

## Background and Rationale

DCG currently has basic terminal output with highlight.rs providing span rendering. Research shows modern Rust CLI tools use libraries like Ratatui, comfy-table, and indicatif to create visually polished, professional-looking terminal interfaces that improve user comprehension and trust.

This epic transforms DCG output from functional to exceptional, making it feel like a premium, well-crafted tool. Visual polish is not just aesthetic—it improves information density and user comprehension, reducing cognitive load when reviewing blocked commands.

## Research Findings (2025-2026)

### Ratatui (v0.30+)
- Crossterm backend is default and cross-platform (Linux/macOS/Windows)
- Widgets: Paragraph, Table, Block, List, Gauge for progress
- Immediate mode rendering - no persistent state
- Perfect for CLI output formatting, not just full TUI apps

### comfy-table (v8.0+)
- ~30μs for basic table rendering
- Supports UTF-8 borders, colors, cell alignment
- Dynamic column width calculation
- Perfect for scan results and stats output

### indicatif (v0.17+)
- Progress bars with ETA calculation
- Spinners with 80+ built-in styles
- Multi-progress bar support
- Perfect for long-running scan operations

### colored (v3.0+)
- Simple color macros for basic output
- Cross-platform with automatic TTY detection
- Already in DCG - can be enhanced

## Example Improved Output

### Denial Message (Before)
```
DENY: rm -rf / is destructive
Pattern: filesystem.recursive-delete-root
```

### Denial Message (After)
```
╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│  ⛔  BLOCKED: Destructive Command Detected                    │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  $ rm -rf /                                                   │
│           ^^^                                                 │
│                                                               │
│  Pattern:  filesystem.recursive-delete-root                   │
│  Severity: CRITICAL                                           │
│                                                               │
│  Why: Recursively deletes the entire filesystem. This is      │
│       irreversible and will destroy your system.              │
│                                                               │
│  Safe alternatives:                                           │
│    • rm -rf ./unwanted-dir  (explicit path)                   │
│    • trash /path            (recoverable deletion)            │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Scan Results (After)
```
╭─ Scan Results ─────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ File                   │ Line │ Severity │ Pattern             │
├────────────────────────┼──────┼──────────┼─────────────────────┤
│ Dockerfile             │   23 │ HIGH     │ docker.system-prune │
│ .github/workflows/ci.y │   45 │ MEDIUM   │ git.force-push      │
│ Makefile               │   12 │ LOW      │ filesystem.chmod    │
╰────────────────────────┴──────┴──────────┴─────────────────────╯
 3 issues found (1 high, 1 medium, 1 low)
```

## Technical Approach

1. Add ratatui, comfy-table, indicatif as dependencies
2. Create src/output/mod.rs module for rich output formatting
3. Implement denial message box renderer
4. Implement scan results table renderer
5. Add progress bar for scan operations
6. Create consistent border/color theme system
7. Ensure TTY detection falls back to plain text gracefully

## Key Files

- Cargo.toml - Add dependencies
- src/output/mod.rs - New: Output formatting module
- src/output/denial.rs - New: Denial message box renderer
- src/output/tables.rs - New: Table renderer for scan/stats
- src/output/progress.rs - New: Progress indicators
- src/output/theme.rs - New: Consistent color/border themes
- src/hook.rs - Integrate new denial renderer
- src/scan.rs - Integrate table and progress renderers

## Dependencies (Crates)

```toml
ratatui = "0.30"           # Rich TUI widgets
comfy-table = "8.0"        # Fast table rendering
indicatif = "0.17"         # Progress bars and spinners
console = "0.15"           # Terminal detection utilities
```

## Performance Considerations

- comfy-table: ~30μs overhead per table (negligible)
- Ratatui rendering: ~50μs for simple paragraphs
- Total budget: <1ms for all rendering (well within perf budget)
- Lazy initialize widgets only when needed
- Skip rendering entirely when --json flag is present

## Graceful Degradation

1. TTY Detection: Check if stdout is a terminal
2. NO_COLOR env: Respect standard for disabling colors
3. Fallback: Plain text output maintains all information
4. CI/CD: Auto-detect and use simple output

## Success Criteria

- Denial messages display in visually appealing box format
- Scan results render in professional table format
- Progress bar shows during long scans (>100 files)
- TTY detection works correctly across platforms
- NO_COLOR respected for accessibility
- No visual regression in non-TTY contexts
- Performance overhead <1ms total

## Synergy with Other Epics

- **Visual Explanations (r97e)**: Explanations display in the box
- **Rule Metrics (1dri)**: Stats use table formatting
- **Scan Extractors (5rbb)**: Results use table + progress

## Notes

This is high priority because visual polish directly impacts user perception and trust. A tool that looks professional is trusted more than one with basic output.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:49:58.171784891-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:49:58.171784891-05:00"}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-advk.10","title":"[TUI] Colorblind-safe palette and high contrast mode","description":"# Task: Colorblind-Safe Palette and High Contrast Mode

## Background

Approximately 8% of men and 0.5% of women have some form of color vision deficiency. DCG should be usable by everyone, which means not relying solely on color to convey information.

## Implementation

### 1. Shape + Color Coding

Never use color alone. Combine with shapes/symbols:

```
✓ ALLOW   (green + checkmark)
⛔ BLOCK   (red + stop sign)
⚠️ WARNING (yellow + triangle)
ℹ️ INFO    (blue + circle)
```

### 2. Colorblind-Safe Palette

Use palette designed for common types:

| Semantic | Default | Protanopia | Deuteranopia | Tritanopia |
|----------|---------|------------|--------------|------------|
| Error    | #FF4444 | #0072B2    | #0072B2      | #D55E00    |
| Warning  | #FFAA00 | #E69F00    | #E69F00      | #CC79A7    |
| Success  | #44FF44 | #009E73    | #009E73      | #56B4E9    |
| Info     | #4444FF | #56B4E9    | #56B4E9      | #009E73    |

### 3. High Contrast Mode

```bash
# Enable via environment
export DCG_HIGH_CONTRAST=1

# Or via config
[output]
high_contrast = true
```

High contrast mode:
- Uses only black/white with bold for emphasis
- Increases border thickness
- Uses ASCII instead of Unicode box drawing

### 4. Theme Configuration

```toml
# ~/.config/dcg/config.toml
[theme]
palette = "default"  # default | colorblind | high-contrast
use_unicode = true   # false for ASCII-only
use_color = true     # false for monochrome
```

### 5. Automatic Detection

Respect standard environment variables:
- NO_COLOR: Disable all colors
- TERM=dumb: ASCII only, no colors
- COLORTERM: Detect 256/truecolor support

## Implementation in Theme System

```rust
pub fn get_palette() -> Palette {
    if env::var("NO_COLOR").is_ok() {
        return Palette::monochrome();
    }
    if env::var("DCG_HIGH_CONTRAST").is_ok() {
        return Palette::high_contrast();
    }
    match config.theme.palette.as_str() {
        "colorblind" => Palette::colorblind_safe(),
        "high-contrast" => Palette::high_contrast(),
        _ => Palette::default(),
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria

- All information conveyed by color also conveyed by shape/symbol
- Colorblind palette available and tested
- High contrast mode works
- NO_COLOR environment variable respected
- Documentation explains accessibility features
- Tested with color blindness simulator

## Testing

- Simulator testing with Sim Daltonism or Coblis
- Screen reader testing for prompt accessibility
- Verify all semantic info has non-color indicator","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T15:24:15.193228923-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T15:24:15.193228923-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.10","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T15:24:15.195029683-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

## Theme System (theme.rs)

```rust
pub struct Theme {
    pub border_style: BorderStyle,
    pub severity_colors: SeverityColors,
    pub accent_color: Color,
}

pub struct SeverityColors {
    pub critical: Color,  // Red
    pub high: Color,      // Orange
    pub medium: Color,    // Yellow
    pub low: Color,       // Blue
}

impl Theme {
    pub fn default() -> Self { ... }
    pub fn no_color() -> Self { ... }  // Plain ASCII, no colors
}
```

## TTY Detection (mod.rs)

```rust
pub fn should_use_rich_output() -> bool {
    // 1. Check --json flag (explicit override)
    // 2. Check NO_COLOR env var
    // 3. Check if stdout is a TTY
    // 4. Check TERM env var for dumb terminals
    console::Term::stdout().is_term() 
        && std::env::var("NO_COLOR").is_err()
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- TTY detection works correctly
- NO_COLOR environment variable respected
- Theme system supports both rich and plain output","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:50:21.115513884-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:50:21.115513884-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:50:21.117473636-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:53:26.078684592-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-advk.3","title":"[TUI] Implement denial message box renderer","description":"Create src/output/denial.rs with a rich denial message renderer.

## Design

The denial box should display:
1. Header with BLOCKED indicator and emoji
2. The command with span highlighting 
3. Pattern name and severity
4. Explanation (from Visual Explanations epic)
5. Safe alternatives as bullet list

## Implementation

```rust
pub struct DenialBox {
    command: String,
    span: HighlightSpan,
    pattern_id: String,
    severity: Severity,
    explanation: Option<String>,
    alternatives: Vec<String>,
}

impl DenialBox {
    pub fn render(&self, theme: &Theme) -> String {
        // Use ratatui Block widget for border
        // Use Paragraph widget for content
        // Fall back to ASCII box if no TTY
    }
    
    pub fn render_plain(&self) -> String {
        // Simple text format for non-TTY contexts
    }
}
```

## Border Style

```
╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│  ⛔  BLOCKED: Destructive Command Detected                    │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Content here...                                              │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

## Integration Point

This integrates with src/hook.rs where denial messages are currently printed.

## Dependencies

- Requires output module (task 2) to be complete
- Works with existing highlight.rs span rendering

## Acceptance Criteria

- Rich box renders correctly with Unicode borders
- Plain fallback works for non-TTY
- Explanation text wraps correctly at terminal width
- Works with existing span highlighting from highlight.rs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:50:22.465040133-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:50:22.465040133-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:50:22.466546671-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:53:28.382198021-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-advk.4","title":"[TUI] Implement scan results table renderer","description":"Create src/output/tables.rs with table rendering using comfy-table.

## Design

Tables should support:
1. Scan results (file, line, severity, pattern)
2. Stats output (rule, hits, allowed, noise%)
3. Pack list (id, name, pattern count)

## Implementation

```rust
use comfy_table::{Table, ContentArrangement, Row, Cell, Color, Attribute};

pub struct ScanResultsTable {
    results: Vec<ScanResult>,
}

impl ScanResultsTable {
    pub fn render(&self, theme: &Theme, max_width: usize) -> String {
        let mut table = Table::new();
        table
            .set_content_arrangement(ContentArrangement::Dynamic)
            .set_width(max_width)
            .set_header(vec!["File", "Line", "Severity", "Pattern"]);
            
        for result in &self.results {
            table.add_row(vec![
                Cell::new(&result.file).fg(Color::White),
                Cell::new(result.line).fg(Color::Cyan),
                severity_cell(&result.severity),
                Cell::new(&result.pattern_id).fg(Color::Yellow),
            ]);
        }
        
        table.to_string()
    }
}

fn severity_cell(severity: &Severity) -> Cell {
    match severity {
        Severity::Critical => Cell::new("CRIT").fg(Color::Red).add_attribute(Attribute::Bold),
        Severity::High => Cell::new("HIGH").fg(Color::DarkRed),
        Severity::Medium => Cell::new("MED").fg(Color::Yellow),
        Severity::Low => Cell::new("LOW").fg(Color::Blue),
    }
}
```

## Table Styles

Support multiple styles:
- Unicode box drawing (default for TTY)
- ASCII (for CI/CD environments)  
- Compact (minimal spacing)
- Markdown (for documentation output)

## Performance

comfy-table renders at ~30μs per table - well within budget.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Scan results render in clean table format
- Column widths adjust to terminal width
- Severity color coding works
- Plain ASCII fallback for non-TTY
- Markdown output option for docs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:51:03.037171936-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:51:03.037171936-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:51:03.082807316-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:53:29.686472434-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-advk.5","title":"[TUI] Implement progress indicators for scans","description":"Create src/output/progress.rs with progress bars and spinners using indicatif.

## Design

Progress indicators for:
1. File scanning (progress bar with file count)
2. Pattern loading (spinner during startup)
3. History analysis (progress for suggest-allowlist)

## Implementation

```rust
use indicatif::{ProgressBar, ProgressStyle, MultiProgress};

pub struct ScanProgress {
    bar: ProgressBar,
}

impl ScanProgress {
    pub fn new(total_files: u64) -> Self {
        let bar = ProgressBar::new(total_files);
        bar.set_style(
            ProgressStyle::default_bar()
                .template("{spinner:.green} [{bar:40.cyan/blue}] {pos}/{len} files ({eta})")
                .unwrap()
                .progress_chars("█▓░")
        );
        Self { bar }
    }
    
    pub fn tick(&self, file_path: &str) {
        self.bar.set_message(file_path.to_string());
        self.bar.inc(1);
    }
    
    pub fn finish_with_message(&self, msg: &str) {
        self.bar.finish_with_message(msg.to_string());
    }
}

pub fn spinner(message: &str) -> ProgressBar {
    let spinner = ProgressBar::new_spinner();
    spinner.set_style(
        ProgressStyle::default_spinner()
            .tick_strings(&["⠋", "⠙", "⠹", "⠸", "⠼", "⠴", "⠦", "⠧", "⠇", "⠏"])
            .template("{spinner:.blue} {msg}")
            .unwrap()
    );
    spinner.set_message(message.to_string());
    spinner
}
```

## Integration Points

- src/scan.rs - Wrap file iteration with progress
- src/suggest.rs - Progress for history analysis
- src/main.rs - Spinner during config loading (if slow)

## Threshold for Progress

Only show progress bar when:
- Scanning >20 files (otherwise too fast to see)
- Operation takes >500ms

## Acceptance Criteria

- Progress bar shows during large scans
- Spinner shows during initialization
- ETA calculation is reasonably accurate
- Progress hidden in non-TTY contexts
- No visual artifacts when complete","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:51:21.138242251-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:51:21.138242251-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:51:21.161429022-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:53:30.956426596-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
